highlight: Long-horizon energy forecasting MAE reduced to 38.4%

subsections.title: Highlight Cards
content: - Long-horizon energy forecasting MAE reduced to 38.4%: Further improved over mainstream SOTA solutions
- 50% lower selection and migration cost: New scenarios can be covered quickly even in zero-shot settings
- Forecasting Demo completed in 3-5 days: Validation cycle compressed to within 1 week
- Estimated 5% reduction in business losses: Improves the credibility of long-horizon warning signals

subsections.title: 2.1 Overview
content: **Business Background**: Long-horizon time-series forecasting is widely used in monitoring and early-warning scenarios for various feature indicators in fields such as energy, enabling people to identify risks and anomalies in time based on forecast trends and make adjustments accordingly.

**Solution**: This case is designed for smart energy scenarios such as electricity, water, and natural gas. It takes historical sequence data of specified features in the scenario as input and outputs forecast values at multiple future time points over a long period. By introducing the latest 2025 time-series model, it captures long-cycle abnormal states and provides a credible basis for energy scheduling and control decisions.

subsections.title: 2.2 Classification Tags
content: **Industry**: Energy & Manufacturing
**Category**: Operations, Risk Control
**Technology Category**: Statistical Machine Learning, Large Language Model (LLM), Operations (Testing & Monitoring), Time-Series Forecasting

subsections.title: 2.3 Implementation Cycle
content: - Demo development: 3-5 days

subsections.title: 2.4 Team Composition
content: - Demo phase: 1 business expert, 1 algorithm engineer

subsections.title: 2.5 Business Pain Points
content: - Manual forecasting is costly and error-prone: Manual forecasting depends on practitioner experience, senior experts are expensive, and continuous tracking and forecasting across multiple indicators is not feasible at the same time; less experienced staff have limited judgment and high error rates

- Poor stability in long-horizon forecasting: Existing models show significantly amplified errors when predicting 720+ future time points, making it difficult to capture key periodic patterns and sudden anomalies and causing early warnings to fail

- Low delivery credibility: Existing technical solutions lack a unified automated benchmarking workflow, and different institutions produce inconsistent indicators and conclusions in comparison reports, complicating technology selection and best-practice decisions

subsections.title: 2.6 Core Functions
content: - Zero-shot long-range forecasting: Stably outputs predictions for 720+ future time points on unseen new business data
- Intelligent ranking and model selection: Automatically compares mainstream time-series forecasting models and generates ranking results to support model selection

subsections.title: 3.1.1 Reasons for Best Practice Selection
content: - Leading accuracy: MAE is reduced to 38.4%, a further 5.2% lower than iTransformer and 3.6% lower than PatchTST.
- Lower migration cost: Zero-shot performance approaches that of full-training solutions, reducing selection and migration cost by 50%.
- Lower business loss: With reduced forecasting error, the solution is estimated to cut losses in energy scheduling and risk control by about 5%.
- Faster launch: Built on a low-code framework, a Demo can be completed in 3-5 days, compressing validation to within 1 week.

subsections.title: 3.1.3 Implementation Details
content: To be released

subsections.title: 3.1.4 Metrics
content: **Core Business Metrics**
- Confidence in solution selection increases by 10% (compared with algorithm researchers) to 70% (compared with non-algorithm practitioners), enabling business staff to make fast and accurate choices
- Selection and migration cost is reduced by 50%
- Mean Absolute Error (MAE) < 0.39, reducing losses caused by long-horizon forecasting errors in the energy field by about 5%
**Core Technical Metrics**
- Mean Absolute Error (MAE) < 0.39: same as the core business metric
